title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard that enables AI applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a common interface. The protocol uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication and supports both local and remote deployment patterns with STDIO and HTTP transports. MCP is essential for building multi-agent AI systems that require tool integration and context management.
writeup: The video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard framework that lets AI agents and applications connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services. It covers the client-server communication model built on JSON-RPC, and the two transport options for local and remote servers: STDIO and HTTP. The relevance is MCP's role as infrastructure for multi-agent systems, with references to frameworks including LangGraph, CrewAI, and Claude Desktop. Key takeaways: the distinction between MCP client and MCP server, and how the protocol standardizes integration across local and remote architectures.
